tavily.answer: In 2025, internal displacements caused by violence or conflict reached a record high, surpassing those caused by natural disasters for the first time, with 32.3 million people displaced due to conflict. In the Red Sea region, Iranian-backed Houthi rebels have vowed to block Israeli ships, claiming responsibility for missile attacks on Israel. Meanwhile, the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo has seen confirmed cases top 1,000 with 254 deaths, and attacks on Ebola treatment centers highlight the challenges faced in the response. The United Nations’ food agencies have warned that acute hunger is set to worsen in 13 global hotspots, including Sudan, South Sudan, Yemen, and Palestine, due to conflict, funding shortages, and climate shocks.
